# Pylon

> Pylon is a Rails-like framework for realtime apps. You define entities, policies, and server functions in TypeScript; Pylon gives you a typed client, live queries, auth, row-level security, jobs, workflows, files, search, and a portable runtime that can run locally, managed, or on your infrastructure.

Pylon is best for apps where two browsers need to see the same thing instantly: dashboards, CRMs, collaborative editors, multiplayer games, chat. Storage is pluggable: SQLite by default, Postgres via `DATABASE_URL`. Schema, policies, functions, and client APIs are identical across both adapters.

Pylon Cloud is the managed path at <https://cloud.pylonsync.com>. It runs the same Pylon app model without requiring teams to operate the infrastructure themselves.

The system has four primitives: entities (typed tables), policies (row-level access rules evaluated as expressions), functions (queries/mutations/actions authored in TypeScript and RPC-called by the client), and live queries (subscriptions that restream results when dependent rows change). All four live in one process.

## Docs

- [Introduction](https://docs.pylonsync.com): What Pylon is, who it's for, how to read the docs.
- [Quickstart](https://docs.pylonsync.com/quickstart): Ship a live-syncing app in five minutes â€” schema, policy, function, live query.
- [Installation](https://docs.pylonsync.com/installation): CLI install, requirements (Bun, Rust for source builds), directory conventions, env vars.

## Core concepts

- [Entities](https://docs.pylonsync.com/concepts/entities): Declaring typed tables â€” `field.string()`, `field.int()`, `field.float()` / `field.number()`, `field.bool()` / `field.boolean()`, `field.datetime()`, `field.richtext()`, `field.id(target)`, plus `.optional()` / `.unique()` modifiers, `.crdt("text")` for collaborative text, and composite indexes.
- [Policies](https://docs.pylonsync.com/concepts/policies): Row-level access rules with expressions referencing `auth.*`, `data.*`, `existing.*`. Omitted actions are denied by default. Server functions bypass policies.
- [Functions](https://docs.pylonsync.com/concepts/functions): Server TypeScript in `functions/*.ts`. `mutation`, `query`, `action`. Validators from `@pylonsync/functions`: `v.string()`, `v.int()`, `v.number()` / `v.float()`, `v.boolean()` / `v.bool()`, `v.id(entity)`, `v.optional(...)`, `v.array(...)`, `v.literal(...)`, `v.object({...})`, `v.union(...)`, `v.any()`. Datetimes are plain `v.datetime()` or `v.string()` holding ISO-8601.
- [Live queries](https://docs.pylonsync.com/concepts/live-queries): How `db.useQuery` subscribes and fan-outs update on writes. Subscriptions respect policies. Use indexed filter columns for fast fan-out.

## Packages

- `@pylonsync/sdk` â€” schema DSL: `entity()`, `field.*`, `policy()`, `buildManifest()`.
- `@pylonsync/functions` â€” server function DSL: `query()`, `mutation()`, `action()`, validators (`v.*`).
- `@pylonsync/react` â€” client hooks: `db.useQuery`, `db.useOne`, `callFn`, `init`, `configureClient`, `storageKey`, `useSession`.
- `@pylonsync/loro` â€” CRDT row hooks: `useLoroDoc`, `useCollabText`, Loro binary-frame decoding.

## Runtime

- Single Rust binary `pylon`. Dev: `pylon dev app.ts` watches schema + functions, runs SQLite on `.pylon/dev.db` by default (or Postgres if `DATABASE_URL` is set), serves HTTP on `:4321`. Production: `pylon start app.ts` â€” same server, no file watcher, blocks on the server thread so process supervisors restart cleanly on fatal errors.
- WebSocket for live queries; HTTP POST `/api/fn/<name>` for function RPC; `/api/entities/<Entity>` for direct CRUD (policy-checked).
- Env vars: `PYLON_DB_PATH`, `PYLON_FILES_DIR`, `PYLON_CORS_ORIGIN`, `PYLON_PORT`, `PYLON_DEV_MODE`, `PYLON_RATE_LIMIT_MAX`, `PYLON_FN_RATE_LIMIT_MAX`.

## Deploy

- **Fly.io** â€” recommended for the architecture. Single binary + SQLite volume + WebSockets is Fly's happy path. LiteFS for multi-region SQLite replication. `fly deploy . --config examples/<app>/fly.toml`. Autostop machines â†’ idle cost near zero.
- **Cloudflare Workers** â€” true edge/$0 idle. WASM adapter + D1 + Durable Objects. Experimental; see `crates/workers/`.
- **Docker / Compose / systemd / SST** â€” `pylon deploy --target docker|compose|systemd|sst` scaffolds config. Any host that runs Docker works.

## Examples (under `examples/`)

- [Todo](https://github.com/pylonsync/pylon/tree/main/examples/todo-app): Minimal reference app â€” schema, policy, function, live query.
- [Swift Todo](https://github.com/pylonsync/pylon/tree/main/examples/swift-todo): SwiftUI iOS/macOS app on the Swift SDK.
- [Chat](https://github.com/pylonsync/pylon/tree/main/examples/chat): Slack-style messaging with presence, typing, tenant isolation.
- [Linear](https://github.com/pylonsync/pylon/tree/main/examples/linear): Issue tracker with teams, cycles, projects, monotonic IDs.
- [Auction House](https://github.com/pylonsync/pylon/tree/main/examples/auction-house): Transactional bids + scheduled settlement.
- [Store](https://github.com/pylonsync/pylon/tree/main/examples/store): 10k-SKU faceted search with live counts.
- [ERP](https://github.com/pylonsync/pylon/tree/main/examples/erp): Multi-tenant B2B fabrication ERP â€” orgs, roles, inventory.
- [Forge](https://github.com/pylonsync/pylon/tree/main/examples/forge): Collaborative 3D scene editor â€” Figma-for-3D.
- [World3D](https://github.com/pylonsync/pylon/tree/main/examples/world3d): Three.js multiplayer avatar world.
- [Arena](https://github.com/pylonsync/pylon/tree/main/examples/arena): Mass-multiplayer 2D dot world â€” scale test.
- [Trade](https://github.com/pylonsync/pylon/tree/main/examples/trade): Market dashboard with live tick fan-out.
- [Bench](https://github.com/pylonsync/pylon/tree/main/examples/bench): In-browser load test with p50/p95/p99 percentiles.

## Optional

- [GitHub](https://github.com/pylonsync/pylon): Source, issues, releases.
- [Changelog](https://github.com/pylonsync/pylon/releases): Per-version notes.
